title: Challenging Mathematical Problems With Elementary Solutions: 001
author: A. M. Yaglom, I. M. Yaglom
publisher: most can be solved with elementary mathematics. In fact
description: Designed for advanced high school students, undergraduates, graduate students, mathematics teachers, and any lover of mathematical challenges, this two-volume set offers a broad spectrum of challenging problems -- ranging from relatively simple to extremely difficult. Indeed, some rank among the finest achievements of outstanding mathematicians. Translated from a well-known Russian work entitled Non-Elementary Problems in an Elementary Exposition, the chief aim of the book is to acquaint the readers with a variety of new mathematical facts, ideas, and methods. And while the majority of the problems represent questions in higher ("non-elementary"") mathematics
notes: all dealing with probability theory and combinatorial analysis. Topics include the representation of integers as sums and products
addedAt: followed by a section giving complete solutions. Answers and hints are given at the end of the book. Ideal as a text
